experiments.description: WebXR immersive-AR session with tap-to-place anchors relative to session origin. Demonstrates browser-native local tracking with honest drift labelling.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: Room-scale only — resets on session restart
experiments.driftNote: Session origin drifts over time. Objects will not remain in place across sessions.
limitations: No GPS — cannot place objects at real-world coordinates
High drift over time without visual re-localisation
experiments.bestUseCase: Testing basic AR anchor placement; indoor demos; device capability validation

experiments.description: Leaflet.js map with live GPS position, accuracy circle, and user-placed POI markers. Includes a compass-bearing panel and optional camera compass overlay.
experiments.driftNote: GPS signal degrades in urban canyons or indoors.
limitations: Map/navigation mode only — objects not placed in 3D AR space
experiments.bestUseCase: Outdoor POI visualisation; GPS accuracy assessment; map + bearing UI prototype

experiments.description: Native Unity AR Foundation session (ARCore/ARKit) with local-world anchors and optional GPS overlay. Baseline for native tracking quality comparison.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: cm-scale local; GPS overlay adds ~5–15 m
experiments.driftNote: Local anchors are stable within a session. GPS overlay drifts independently.
limitations: Local anchors do not persist without cloud anchors
GPS overlay accuracy limited by hardware
experiments.bestUseCase: Indoor AR prototyping; local anchor quality benchmarking
deviceRequirements: Android ARCore device or iOS ARKit device

experiments.description: ARCore Geospatial API providing WGS-84 anchors at ~10–30 cm accuracy outdoors. Requires Google VPS streetscape coverage.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: 10–30 cm horizontal where streetscape coverage exists
experiments.driftNote: Accuracy degrades significantly outside Google streetscape coverage areas.
experiments.bestUseCase: Outdoor AR with persistent geo-anchored content in covered areas

experiments.description: Niantic Lightship VPS for sub-metre localisation at pre-scanned wayspots. Enables persistent AR anchors at known real-world locations.
experiments.driftNote: Accuracy only guaranteed at pre-scanned Niantic wayspot locations.
limitations: Requires Niantic Lightship SDK — native build only
VPS only works at pre-scanned locations
Niantic API key and developer account required
experiments.bestUseCase: Persistent, shareable AR experiences at specific real-world landmarks

experiments.description: High-precision survey coordinate system AR. Anchors placed using GIS/local grid coordinates for engineering, construction, or survey use cases.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: Dependent on survey control point accuracy (mm–cm with RTK GPS)
experiments.driftNote: Accuracy is bounded by quality of the control point survey data.
experiments.bestUseCase: AEC / engineering AR overlay on surveyed sites; utility mapping
